forecast-period.text: A low and trough over northeast SA will continue to move northeastwards clearing the State by Thursday. A ridge of high pressure persists to the south.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Southeasterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Port Neill to Port Victoria.
1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres south of Port Neill to Port Victoria.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Mostly clear.
South to southeasterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots north of Cowell to Wallaroo in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, increasing to 1 to 2 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Partly cloudy.
Southeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly 15 to 20 knots in the morning.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres by early evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Mostly sunny.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending south to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the day then tending south to southeasterly during the evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon or evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Mostly sunny.
